behaviors.description: The embedding vector produced for a given query string has the correct dimensionality for the vector database index.
behaviors.justification: Failure is at the embedding-model output boundary: the behavior asserts properties of the real model output (vector length), not just local wrapper logic. A stub can hide real failures such as provider/model-version dimensionality drift. Considered Integration, but rejected because the behavior text requires the vector actually "produced" by the embedding model, so fake outputs disqualify lower-level confidence.
behaviors.test_description: Call the real embedding model for representative queries and assert returned vector length exactly matches the live index dimension expected by the vector DB configuration.

behaviors.description: The similarity search returns results ordered by descending cosine similarity score.
behaviors.justification: This is most likely to fail at the API↔vector-DB boundary (metric/index configuration, score semantics, tie behavior), which mocks often hide. Real vector DB behavior is needed to catch misconfigured cosine vs dot-product or backend ordering differences. Considered Integration, but rejected because behavior text is about actual similarity search result ordering, which is commonly backend-dependent.
behaviors.test_description: Run queries against a real vector DB instance seeded with controlled embeddings, then assert returned scores are monotonic descending and ordering matches cosine similarity expectations.

behaviors.description: The LLM re-ranking step changes the order of at least some results compared to the raw similarity ranking when the query has nuanced intent.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real LLM judgment for nuanced intent; deterministic stubs would trivially pass/lock ordering and miss true reranking failures. This is a single agentic decision point, not a multi-agent pipeline. Considered Workflow, but rejected because behavior describes one LLM rerank step, not multiple agentic stages.
behaviors.test_description: For nuanced benchmark queries, compare raw similarity ranking vs ranking produced by the real reranker LLM and assert at least one meaningful position change occurs in a minimum fraction of cases.

behaviors.description: A query with no close matches in the database returns an empty result set rather than low-relevance noise.
behaviors.justification: Primary risk is module handoff/policy logic (similarity scores + thresholding + result filtering). This can be caught with wired local components and controlled vectors; no real OS/network/subprocess is inherently required, and correctness need not depend on real LLM output quality. Considered Agentic, but rejected because behavior text focuses on empty-set threshold behavior for "no close matches," not model semantic quality.
behaviors.test_description: With a local seeded vector store and deterministic embeddings/test vectors intentionally far from all indexed items, execute search through service layers and assert the final response is an empty list when scores stay below threshold.

behaviors.description: The end-to-end latency from receiving the query to returning re-ranked results stays below 2 seconds for the median case.
behaviors.justification: This is full-pipeline performance across multiple agentic steps in sequence (embedding model call + LLM rerank) plus service orchestration. Latency behavior is emergent across chained steps and real dependencies, so lower isolated levels can miss real bottlenecks. Considered System, but rejected because the behavior explicitly spans multi-step agentic pipeline end-to-end.
behaviors.test_description: Execute production-like end-to-end requests through the real API with real embedding and reranking calls over a representative query set, measure total response times, and assert median latency (p50) remains under 2.0 seconds.
